title: Coarse Menger Conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
context_text: This conjecture is a coarse analogue of Menger's classical 1927 theorem, motivated by coarse graph theory (large-scale geometric structure of graphs) and complexity-theoretic questions about finding vertex-disjoint paths at distance at least $d$. Both groups of authors proved the $k=2$ case; the $k\geq 3$ case was open. The case $d=3$ is of special interest because truth for $d=3$ implies truth for all $d\geq 3$ (via the $d$th power of $G$).
attributed_to: Albrechtsen, Huynh, Jacobs, Knappe and Wollan; and independently Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu
paper_contribution: Provides an explicit counterexample with $d=k=3$ and maximum degree at most three, showing the conjecture is false for all $k\geq 3$; also gives a simpler proof of the $k=2$ case.
paper_title: A counterexample to the coarse Menger conjecture
